SEARCH
Package io.stimulustech.filequeue

Class FileQueue<T extends FileQueueItem>

java.lang.Object
io.stimulustech.filequeue.FileQueue<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of FileQueueItem managed by this queue
public final class FileQueue<T extends FileQueueItem> extends Object
FileQueue is a fast, efficient, persistent queue written in Java and backed by H2 MVStore. It supports retry behavior for items that cannot be processed immediately. Because the queue is persistent, processing resumes where it left off after a restart. Refer to the FileQueue GitHub repository for more information.
For an example, refer to io.stimulustech.filequeue.FileQueueTest.

For higher performance, FileQueue transfers queued items directly to consumers without hitting the database when consumers are available. If all consumers are busy, FileQueue automatically persist queued items to the database.

FileQueue supports both fixed and exponential backoff retry strategies.

Implementation strategy:
1) Create a Jackson-serializable POJO by extending FileQueueItem.
2) Implement a consumer by implementing Consumer.
3) Implement consume(item) with your processing logic.
4) Call config() to configure FileQueue.
5) Call startQueue(config) to start processing.
6) Call stopQueue() to stop processing.

Example usage:

 FileQueue queue = FileQueue.fileQueue();
 ExecutorService executor = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors());
 FileQueue.Config config = FileQueue.config(queueName, queuePath, TestFileQueueItem.class, new TestConsumer(), executor)
                           .maxQueueSize(MAXQUEUESIZE)
                           .retryDelayAlgorithm(QueueProcessor.RetryDelayAlgorithm.EXPONENTIAL)
                           .retryDelay(RETRYDELAY)
                           .maxRetryDelay(MAXRETRYDELAY)
                           .maxTries(0)
                           .persistRetryDelay(PERSISTENTRETRYDELAY);
 queue.startQueue(config);
 for (int i = 0; i < ROUNDS; i++)
     queue.queueItem(new TestFileQueueItem(i));
 // When finished, call stopQueue.
 queue.stopQueue();

For a full example, see io.stimulustech.filequeue.FileQueueTest.

Author:
Jamie Band, Valentin Popov

  • Constructor Details

    • FileQueue

      public FileQueue()
      Creates a new, uninitialised FileQueue instance. Call startQueue(Config) to begin processing.

  • Method Details

    • startQueue

      public void startQueue(FileQueue.Config<T> config) throws IOException, IllegalStateException, IllegalArgumentException, InterruptedException
      Starts the queue engine using the supplied configuration. Registers a JVM shutdown hook to ensure the queue is cleanly stopped on exit.

      Executor lifecycle: the ExecutorService embedded in config is owned by the caller. It must remain running for the full lifetime of the queue. Shutting down the executor while the queue is running will cause tasks to be rejected and pushed to the persistent store instead of being executed. Call stopQueue() first, then shut down the executor.

      Parameters:
      config - queue configuration; call config(String, Path, Class, Consumer, ExecutorService) to build one
      Throws:
      IOException - if the persistent store cannot be opened
      InterruptedException - if the thread is interrupted during initialisation
      IllegalArgumentException - if config is null or any configuration value is invalid
      IllegalStateException - if the queue has already been started

    • getConfig

      public FileQueue.Config<T> getConfig() throws IllegalStateException
      Returns the active queue configuration.

      Warning: the returned object is the live, mutable FileQueue.Config instance passed to startQueue(Config). Calling setters on it after the queue has started does not affect the running processor — the QueueProcessor was already built from a snapshot of the config values. The only property that can be changed at runtime is the queue size, via setMaxQueueSize(int).

      Returns:
      currently active FileQueue.Config
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the queue has not been started

    • stopQueue

      public void stopQueue()
      Stops the queue engine and blocks until all in-flight items have either been processed or persisted back to the on-disk store.

      Items that are mid-consume() when this is called will run to completion before this method returns. Any item that returns Consumer.Result.FAIL_REQUEUE or throws IllegalStateException during that final processing round is persisted to the on-disk store and will be retried on the next start.

      The JVM shutdown hook registered by startQueue(io.stimulustech.filequeue.FileQueue.Config<T>) is removed. Calling this method on a queue that is not running is a no-op.

      After this method returns, the caller should shut down the ExecutorService that was supplied in the configuration.

    • config

      public static <T extends FileQueueItem> FileQueue.Config<T> config(String queueName, Path queuePath, Class<T> type, Consumer<? super T> consumer, ExecutorService executorService)
      Creates a FileQueue.Config with all mandatory fields set, ready for further customisation via its fluent setter methods.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the queue item type
      Parameters:
      queueName - unique name for the queue
      queuePath - writable path where the queue database will be stored
      type - concrete FileQueueItem subclass to be queued
      consumer - callback invoked to process each dequeued item
      executorService - thread pool used to execute processing tasks
      Returns:
      a new FileQueue.Config ready to be passed to startQueue(Config)

    • queueItem

      public <T1 extends T> void queueItem(@Nonnull T1 fileQueueItem, int acquireWait, @Nonnull TimeUnit acquireWaitUnit) throws IOException, InterruptedException
      Queues an item for delivery, waiting up to acquireWait for a free slot. If no slot becomes available within the timeout an IllegalStateException is thrown.
      Type Parameters:
      T1 - the concrete item type, must extend T
      Parameters:
      fileQueueItem - item for queuing; must not be null
      acquireWait - maximum time to wait for a free slot; must be >= 0
      acquireWaitUnit - time unit for acquireWait; must not be null
      Throws:
      IOException - if the item could not be saved to the persistent store
      InterruptedException - if the calling thread is interrupted while waiting
      NullPointerException - if fileQueueItem or acquireWaitUnit is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if acquireWait is negative
      IllegalStateException - if the queue is not started, or no slot was available within the timeout

    • queueItem

      public void queueItem(T fileQueueItem) throws IOException, InterruptedException
      Queues an item for delivery, blocking indefinitely until a free slot is available.
      Parameters:
      fileQueueItem - item for queuing; must not be null
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if fileQueueItem is null
      IllegalStateException - if the queue has not been started
      IOException - if the item could not be serialized or persisted
      InterruptedException - if the calling thread is interrupted while waiting

    • getQueueSize

      public long getQueueSize() throws IllegalStateException
      Returns the number of items currently persisted in the on-disk queue store.
      Returns:
      number of items in the persistent queue
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the queue has not been started

    • setMaxQueueSize

      public void setMaxQueueSize(int queueSize)
      Adjusts the maximum number of items the queue will hold concurrently. Takes effect immediately on both the active configuration and the running processor.
      Parameters:
      queueSize - new maximum size; must be >= 0
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if queueSize is negative

    • destroy

      public static void destroy()
      Shuts down the shared processor thread pool. Call once at application exit to release background threads managed by QueueProcessor.

    • fileQueue

      public static <T extends FileQueueItem> FileQueue<T> fileQueue()
      Factory method that creates a new, uninitialised FileQueue instance. Equivalent to new FileQueue<>().
      Type Parameters:
      T - the queue item type
      Returns:
      a new FileQueue ready to be started

    • availablePermits

      public int availablePermits()
      Returns the number of permits currently available, i.e. the number of additional items that may be submitted to the queue without blocking.
      Returns:
      number of available queue slots
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the queue has not been started
